meta.title: Can your agent land its own PR?

nodes.question: Where is your agent running?
nodes.context: The walls differ by surface: a workflow's ambient token, a platform-gated agent session, and a local CLI with a PAT each fail in their own characteristic ways.

options.label: In a GitHub Actions workflow
In an agent session (MCP-gated platform)
Local CLI + PAT — opening PRs works; landing them is the problem

nodes.question: Does `gh pr create` (createPullRequest) die in your workflow?

options.label: Yes — a GraphQL error says Actions is "not permitted"
I want the job to SURVIVE that wall instead of failing
No — the PR opens fine; merging is the problem

nodes.question: What does the GitHub tooling in your agent session say?

options.label: A 403 says access "is not enabled for this session"
An org admin must connect the app — or `gh` is missing entirely
No wall here — the PR opens; merging is the problem

nodes.question: PR open — can the agent MERGE it?
nodes.context: Opening a PR is the easy half. Landing it hits a different class of walls: reviewer-safety classifiers, branch protection, and token scope.

options.label: It tries to merge its OWN PR — and gets refused
I want agent PRs to land automatically on green
Branch protection / required checks are blocking it
Token or API calls 403 — or report state that seems wrong

nodes.question: How should agent PRs land without a human click?

options.label: A repo workflow arms GitHub-native auto-merge at PR open
No enabler installed — or the PR is ALREADY green

nodes.question: Branch protection & required checks — which symptom?

options.label: Pushing straight to main is rejected (GH013)
The PR sits at mergeable_state=blocked while a check pends
A required check never reports at all — merge jammed forever
ZERO required checks are configured and auto-merge is on

nodes.question: Token & scope walls — which one did you hit?

options.label: Branch deletion / tag push / release create returns 403
Issues my automation opens never trigger the label routines
The PR state my tooling reads seems stale or wrong

nodes.verdict: The repo/org toggle is off — GitHub refuses the workflow's own token at PR-creation time. Your workflow code is fine.
nodes.error: GraphQL: GitHub Actions is not permitted to create or approve pull requests (createPullRequest)
nodes.fix: Settings → Actions → General → Workflow permissions → check "Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests" (owner console only — no workflow content can change it). A PAT stored as an Actions secret bypasses it too. Proof of fix from this fleet: after the toggle flipped, run 29202721928 created PR #194 as github-actions[bot] and it auto-merged.
sources: docs/CAPABILITIES.md@2a4c78a (2026-07-12 wall entry — runs 29167034060 and 29184552812 both died at this exact line)
docs/owner/OWNER-ACTIONS.md@2a4c78a (Decided row M — run 29202721928 → PR #194, merged_by github-actions[bot])

nodes.verdict: Design the workflow to degrade — a refused PR create must not fail the job when the work itself succeeded.
nodes.fix: Copy the landing ladder: (1) try a direct push to main; (2) on ruleset rejection push a fallback branch — the data is safe on the remote regardless of what follows; (3) if PR creation is refused, exit 0 on purpose and write a run summary carrying the compare link so any session (or the owner) can open the PR by hand.

nodes.verdict: That 403 is the platform proxy's per-session gate, not GitHub — the session was never granted this repository.
nodes.error: {"message":"GitHub access to this repository is not enabled for this session. Use add_repo to request access."}
nodes.fix: Request repo access for the session (add_repo). And know the gates are separate: plain `git push` to a branch can still work while the API gate is closed — push the branch and hand PR-open/merge to a session that has the tooling. Never record "pushed" without proof (push exit 0 AND git ls-remote showing the commit).
sources: docs/CAPABILITIES.md@2a4c78a (2026-07-10 append-log entries — verbatim 403 + the git-proxy-is-separate finding)

nodes.verdict: An org-level install gate — no per-session request fixes it. And agent sandboxes often ship without the gh CLI at all.
nodes.error: An org admin must connect the Claude GitHub App for this organization.
nodes.fix: An organization admin has to connect/install the GitHub App — queue it as an owner action rather than retrying. Inside the sandbox, `gh: command not found` is normal: use the platform's API/MCP tooling instead of shelling out to gh.

nodes.verdict: Safety classifiers terminally refuse author-side merges — this is a wall, not a flaky call to retry or re-prompt around.
nodes.error: [Self-Approval] The sub-agent prompt instructs a REST squash merge of PR #68, which this session's own sub-agent authored — merging one's own PR defeats two-party review (also [Merge Without Review])
nodes.fix: Land the merge server-side (GitHub-native auto-merge armed by a repo workflow, gated on the required check) or have a non-author do it. Two-party review is the point of the refusal; route around the AUTHOR, not the classifier.

nodes.verdict: The enabler pattern works in production: a repo workflow arms GitHub-native squash auto-merge on non-draft agent PRs the moment they open, and the required check does the gating.
nodes.fix: Copy its two guards. Guard 1: count required status-check CONTEXTS (not rules) and refuse to arm on zero — with no required check, arming merges a PR INSTANTLY. Guard 2: a `do-not-automerge` label carve-out re-read FRESH from the API after a 15s sleep — the event payload snapshots labels at open time, and an MCP-created PR gets its label in a second call right after create (the stale-payload race).

nodes.verdict: No enabler installed? A plain GITHUB_TOKEN workflow that squash-merges on green still lands PRs — on 2026-07-11 only 3 of 13 fleet repos had the enabler; the rest could only park PRs READY and green.
nodes.fix: Add a merge-on-green workflow — and give it a direct-squash fallback: GitHub REJECTS arming auto-merge on an already-clean PR (nothing left to wait on), so `gh pr merge --auto --squash` must fall back to a plain `gh pr merge --squash`, which only succeeds if the ruleset's required check is already green — GitHub gates it, not the script.
sources: .github/workflows/host-automerge-extras.yml@2a4c78a (arm-on-open + sweep jobs, both with the direct-squash fallback)

nodes.verdict: A GH013 push-to-main rejection is the ruleset WORKING — PR-only main is by design, not a bug to code around.
nodes.fix: Land through a PR. Workflows that generate data should expect the rejection and degrade to a fallback branch on it — this fleet's daily bake tries main first, treats the GH013 as expected, then pushes its bake/ branch.
sources: docs/CAPABILITIES.md@2a4c78a (2026-07-12 wall entry — "ruleset-blocked from pushing main (GH013, expected)")

nodes.verdict: A pending required check reads as mergeable_state=blocked — verified live on PR #18 while the required `quality` check pended.
nodes.fix: Wait for the required check to report; the state flips on green. Don't fight `blocked` with merge retries — it is the ruleset holding the door, and every retry is wasted quota.
sources: docs/owner/OWNER-ACTIONS.md@2a4c78a (Decided row A — owner-configured ruleset, verified live via PR #18)

nodes.verdict: A required context that never reports stays pending FOREVER and jams the merge — the classic paths-ignore trap.
nodes.fix: Never paths-ignore a required check. Give it a fast lane instead: this repo's required `quality` job detects a control-only diff and short-circuits GREEN in-job, so the required context always reports — deliberately NOT a paths-ignore, because a required context that never reports stays pending forever and jams the merge.
sources: .github/workflows/quality.yml@2a4c78a (control fast lane comment — the fleet-protocol heartbeat-lane lesson, 2026-07-09)

nodes.verdict: Zero required checks + auto-merge is an instant-merge hazard: arming a PR with nothing to wait on merges it on the spot.
nodes.fix: Make a real check (this repo: `quality`) REQUIRED on the base branch before any workflow arms auto-merge — and in the arming workflow, count required status-check CONTEXTS and refuse to arm when the count is zero, rather than inverting the gate.

nodes.verdict: Some git operations 403 on EVERY path from an agent environment: branch deletion (git push :branch AND the API), and tag push / release create through the git proxy.
nodes.fix: For releases: `workflow_dispatch` a release workflow with a version input — the tag is created in-Actions, proven repeatedly fleet-wide after direct tag pushes 403'd. For branch deletion: no agent-side path — the owner deletes by hand or enables "Automatically delete head branches".
sources: docs/CAPABILITIES.md@2a4c78a (Walls — verified blocked: tag push / release create; branch deletion)

nodes.verdict: Issues authored by an app/integration token do NOT fire label-triggered routines — the automation chain dead-ends silently.
nodes.fix: Author automation-triggering issues with a PAT (a real user identity), not the app token — label routines only fire for user-authored events.

nodes.verdict: MCP PR-state reads can run ~25 minutes stale — acting on a stale "open" or "merged" is how agents double-land work or abandon a PR that already merged.
nodes.fix: Cross-check with a `git fetch` (or a direct API read) before acting on PR state — treat the cached answer as a hint, never as ground truth for an irreversible action.
